Value: Safety of calcium and vitamin D supplements, a randomized controlled trial
None of the authors had a conflict of interest to declare.
Subjects were asked to refrain from taking calcium and vitamin D supplements.

Data Field: Loss to Follow-up (n per arm if reported)
Value: 38 (18 in high dose D group, 20 in low dose D group)

Data Field: Baseline Stone Size or Burden (if reported)

Value: High Dose Vitamin D3 + Calcium Carbonate
10,000 IU/day Vitamin D3 + 1200 mg/day CaCO3 supplement
Standard Dose Vitamin D3 + Calcium Carbonate (600 IU/day)

Value: Hypercalciuria (24-hour urine calcium excretion over 250 mg)
Constipation (1 in each group reported as the reason for dropout)

Value: 10 (5 in high dose group, 5 in low dose group)
37 patients had 64 events in high D group; 38 patients had 67 events in low D group

Value: No (Mixed-effects models used to handle missing data)
The study was designed to compare the RDA (600 IU) vs the Endocrine Society's suggested UL (10,000 IU) for Vitamin D while maintaining calcium at its UL (2000 mg/day total intake).
